codeReview.summary: This fix closes the prior blocking gap. Android now has a real `EnboxFlagSecure` bridge that toggles `FLAG_SECURE` on the UI thread, the package registration exposes that module to React Native, the JS shim canonicalizes onto the single `EnboxFlagSecure` name, and `RecoveryPhraseScreen` still invokes the shim on mount/unmount. The new integration test covers the Android NativeModules call-through for the recovery-phrase flow. I did not find a blocking or non-blocking functional bug in the shipped fix.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The worker marked `skillFeedback.followedProcedure: true`, but the transcript skeleton does not show the required context reads or red-first test flow from `mobile-feature-worker`. The skill's honesty gate is not being enforced reliably.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: `.factory/skills/mobile-feature-worker/SKILL.md:20-29` requires reading `architecture.md` (and `environment.md` when running install/test flows) before claiming `followedProcedure: true`, and `SKILL.md:66-73` requires a failing test before implementation. The `worker-transcripts.jsonl` entry for session `e3fc5473-3a11-4250-b875-2da6270d2fb6` shows reads of `mission.md`, `AGENTS.md`, `validation-contract.md`, `.factory/services.yaml`, and source files, but no reads of `.factory/library/architecture.md`, `.factory/library/environment.md`, or `.factory/library/user-testing.md`; it also shows `Create FlagSecureModule.kt` / `Edit NativeModulesPackage.kt` / `Create src/lib/native/flag-secure.ts` before `Create src/features/auth/screens/__tests__/recovery-phrase-screen.flag-secure-native.test.tsx`, with the first feature-specific Jest run only afterward.

sharedStateObservations.observation: Mission guidance currently states that native modules should follow the existing TurboModule/codegen pattern, but this feature legitimately added a small Android-only legacy bridge. The shared conventions should document that exception explicitly so future workers are not forced to guess whether `ReactContextBaseJavaModule` is acceptable.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: `/home/liran/.factory/missions/08245e86-9e3a-49bb-ba11-c4ec0804b327/AGENTS.md:47` says native modules should match the `NativeSecureStorage` / `NativeCrypto` codegen pattern, while `android/app/src/main/java/org/enbox/mobile/nativemodules/FlagSecureModule.kt:33-75` implements a plain `ReactContextBaseJavaModule` and `android/app/src/main/java/org/enbox/mobile/nativemodules/NativeModulesPackage.kt:45-54` registers it with `isTurboModule = false`, exactly as the feature description required.

summary: Reviewed the prior failure, handoff, transcript skeleton, skill file, and commit `097f1c5`. The missing Android `FLAG_SECURE` bridge is now implemented and wired into the recovery-phrase flow: `FlagSecureModule` is registered under `EnboxFlagSecure`, the shim uses only that canonical name, `RecoveryPhraseScreen` still toggles the shim on mount/unmount, and the new integration test proves Android `NativeModules.EnboxFlagSecure.activate/deactivate` are reached. I did not find a new functional defect in this fix.
